fundamentals.name: Roundhill Memory ETF
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: The fund will generally seek to invest primarily in the equity securities of “Memory Companies,” but may also seek exposure to Memory Companies through derivative instruments, such as swap agreements and forward contracts. Under normal circumstances, it invests at least 80% of its net assets (plus borrowings for investment purposes) in equity securities or instruments (i.e., swap agreements or forward contracts) that provide exposure to Memory Companies. The fund is non-diversified.

news.paragraph: Micron Technology (MU), a key player in AI memory, has joined the $1 trillion market cap club, fueled by a rally in AI-related stocks. The company's valuation surge follows positive commentary from former President Trump and significant investor interest, with some analysts exploring leveraged ETF strategies around AI chip stocks. This milestone highlights the strong demand and investor optimism surrounding AI infrastructure and memory solutions.

headlines.title: This AI ETF's Price Nearly Doubled in a Few Weeks. Now Wall Street Wants to Add Leverage.
